title: Cream cheese rugelach
subtitle: Flaky cream cheese pastry rolled with jam and walnuts
excerpt: Twenty-four small crescent pastries made from a cream cheese and butter dough, filled with apricot jam and walnuts before rolling. The dough is remarkably easy to handle after chilling and bakes to a layered, flaky crescent that keeps for three days.
sourceNotes: Rugelach are an Ashkenazi Jewish pastry documented in pre-1928 American Jewish community cookbooks and Central European Jewish household cookery texts. The cream cheese dough form was popularised in the United States during the early twentieth century; recipes appear in pre-1928 American Jewish women's organisation cooking guides and community newspapers available through the American Jewish Historical Society archives. Cross-referenced with pre-1928 Eastern European Jewish household baking literature.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze shaped and unbaked. Bake from frozen at the same temperature, adding 5 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Dough can be refrigerated for up to 3 days before rolling.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven. Conventional 190°C. Watch from 18 minutes; they can brown quickly at the ends.

glossaryTerms.definition: A pastry made with equal parts butter, cream cheese, and flour. The cream cheese provides fat and a slight tang, and its moisture creates a tender, flaky texture when the dough is chilled and rolled. It is more forgiving than puff pastry or shortcrust: it handles well cold, can be re-chilled if it softens, and does not shrink excessively when baked. The dough must be well chilled before rolling to prevent it sticking and to ensure clean, sharp layers when cut.

content.text: Rugelach are small Ashkenazi pastries rolled from

: a mixture of butter, cream cheese, and flour that bakes into layers. The filling is a thin spread of apricot jam and chopped walnuts. Twenty-four crescents. The dough needs 2 hours in the fridge before rolling, so make it ahead.

Pulse or rub the cold butter into the flour, sugar, and salt until the mixture resembles rough breadcrumbs. Add the cold cream cheese and mix until a rough dough just comes together. Divide in half, flatten into discs, wrap in cling film, and refrigerate for at least 2 hours.

Heat the oven to 180°C fan (190°C conventional). Mix the chopped walnuts, cinnamon, and 30 g of caster sugar together. On a lightly floured surface, roll one disc of dough into a circle about 30 cm across. Spread half the jam over the surface, then scatter half the walnut mixture. Cut into 12 wedges (like a pizza). Roll each wedge from the wide end to the point and curve slightly into a crescent. Repeat with the second disc. Arrange on lined baking trays and brush with beaten egg.
Bake for 22 to 25 minutes, until golden and set. Cool on the trays for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack. Serve warm or at room temperature.
